description: Expand ccb_investigation from 4 tasks (scoring 0.96-0.985 — near ceiling) to 12 tasks with deeper causal chains, larger search spaces, multi-component interactions, and historical regression hunts. Same output format and scoring as existing tasks.

userStories.title: Create inv-deep-001: Deep causal chain in Envoy filter pipeline
userStories.description: As a benchmark developer, I want an investigation task with a 4-5 hop causal chain. Symptom appears in Envoy's access log (wrong response code) but root cause is in a filter 4 layers back in the filter chain. Agent must trace through 4+ packages to find root cause.
acceptanceCriteria: benchmarks/ccb_investigation/inv-deep-001/ has complete task files (task.toml, instruction.md, environment/Dockerfile, tests/test.sh, tests/ground_truth.json)
task.toml has category=deep_causal_chain, language=cpp, difficulty=hard, time_limit_sec=1200
instruction.md presents a symptom and asks for root cause investigation
ground_truth.json has 8-12 required_findings (more than existing tasks' 5-8)
ground_truth.json causal_chain has 4-5 ordered steps (more than existing 2-3)
Expected baseline score: 0.3-0.5 (significantly harder than current 0.9+)
Dockerfile pins to a commit where the bug exists
Task registered in configs/selected_benchmark_tasks.json and investigation_2config.sh
userStories.notes: Choose a real or realistic Envoy bug where: symptom is in access log → caused by wrong header in router → caused by filter modifying header → caused by config parsing bug → caused by proto default value issue. Use Sourcegraph to find realistic file paths.

userStories.title: Create inv-deep-002: Deep causal chain in Istio control plane
userStories.description: As a benchmark developer, I want a deep causal chain task in Istio. Symptom: Envoy sidecar gets wrong route config. Root cause: Pilot's config translation has a bug 4 hops back in the translation pipeline.
acceptanceCriteria: causal_chain has 4-5 ordered steps through pilot/pkg/xds/ → pilot/pkg/networking/ → pilot/pkg/model/ → pilot/pkg/config/
userStories.notes: Based on real Istio bug (PR #38088/issue #38082): merged DestinationRules lose contributing DR metadata, causing xDS push to be silently skipped. 5-hop chain: mergeDestinationRule → consolidatedDestRules metadata loss → SidecarScope.AddConfigDependencies registers only one ConfigKey → DependsOnConfig returns false → push skipped. Pre-fix commit: f8c9b973.

userStories.title: Create inv-deep-003: Deep causal chain in Terraform
userStories.description: As a benchmark developer, I want a deep causal chain task in Terraform. Symptom: terraform apply creates wrong resource. Root cause is in state management interacting with graph evaluation 4 hops back.
acceptanceCriteria: language=go, 4-5 hop chain through internal/command/ → internal/terraform/ → internal/states/ → internal/providers/
userStories.notes: Based on real Terraform bug (PR #34567/issues #34144, #34323): schema-sensitive attributes not applied to provider responses from ReadResource/PlanResourceChange, causing phantom diffs and incomplete sensitive_attributes in state. 4-hop chain: provider response (unmarked) → node_resource_abstract_instance.go (only prior-state marks applied) → state serialization (incomplete AttrSensitivePaths) → evaluate.go (compensating schema marks create asymmetry → phantom diffs). Pre-fix commit: 9658f9df.

userStories.title: Create inv-interaction-001: Multi-component race condition in Kubernetes
userStories.description: As a benchmark developer, I want an investigation task where the bug emerges from interaction between 3 Kubernetes controllers. No single controller is buggy — the bug only appears when scheduler + node controller + kubelet interact under specific timing.
acceptanceCriteria: instruction.md describes the emergent failure WITHOUT naming the interacting components
ground_truth.json required_findings: must identify ALL 3 interacting components
ground_truth.json causal_chain: must describe the specific timing/ordering that triggers the bug
Expected baseline score: 0.2-0.4 (interaction bugs are the hardest class)
userStories.notes: Based on real K8s bug (PR #120816, commit f718096): processTaintBaseEviction receives stale observedReadyCondition instead of currentReadyCondition, delaying NoExecute taint by one monitoring cycle. 3-component interaction: node lifecycle controller (detection + stale param) + scheduler taint toleration plugin (allows scheduling during delay) + taint eviction controller (evicts after delayed taint). Pre-fix commit: f718096~1.

userStories.title: Create inv-interaction-002: Multi-component interaction in Cilium
userStories.description: As a benchmark developer, I want a multi-component interaction task in Cilium. Bug emerges from interaction between eBPF datapath + userspace agent + Kubernetes watcher under specific conditions (e.g., rapid policy updates).
userStories.notes: Based on real Cilium bug (PR #42049, commit 696017a): Identity allocator's OnListDone closes listDone before SelectorCache processes events, causing GetSelections() to return incomplete identity set during endpoint regeneration. 3-component interaction: identity allocator (pkg/allocator/) + SelectorCache (pkg/policy/) + endpoint regeneration (pkg/endpoint/). Pre-fix commit: 1ded914d.

userStories.title: Create inv-interaction-003: Multi-component interaction in Kafka
userStories.description: As a benchmark developer, I want a multi-component interaction task in Kafka. Bug from producer + broker + consumer interaction (e.g., rebalance during produce causes message loss).
userStories.notes: Based on real Kafka bug (KAFKA-14024, PR #12349, commit d8541b20): ConsumerCoordinator's onJoinPrepare initiates async offset commits during cooperative rebalance, but completion flag never updated, causing repeated commits on each poll() and consumer ejection after timeout. 3-component interaction: ConsumerCoordinator (rebalance + offset commit) + poll() loop (triggers rebalance) + auto-commit mechanism (onJoinPrepare callback). Pre-fix commit: d8541b20~1.

userStories.title: Create inv-regression-001b: Historical regression hunt in Prometheus
userStories.description: As a benchmark developer, I want a task where the agent must use commit history to find when/why a regression was introduced in Prometheus. Dockerfile provides repo at post-regression commit. Agent must find the regressing commit SHA, explain what changed, and why it broke things.
acceptanceCriteria: benchmarks/ccb_investigation/inv-regression-001b/ has complete task files (use -001b to avoid conflict with existing inv-regression-001)
instruction.md describes the regression symptom but NOT when it was introduced
ground_truth.json required_findings include: regressing commit SHA, changed function, mechanism of regression
ground_truth.json causal_chain: commit X changed function Y in way Z → broke assumption W → regression manifests as symptom S
Expected baseline score: 0.2-0.4 (baseline agents lack commit search)
MCP advantage: commit_search + diff_search + compare_revisions are essential
userStories.notes: Based on real Prometheus bug (commit 52630ad): head posting optimization removed sorting step when only one block exists, causing full head scan before filtering instead of using index optimizations. For high-cardinality metrics, this caused 3-5x slowdown in recording rule evaluation (200s → 3000s). Fixed in PR #7051 (commit 635fff9).

userStories.title: Create inv-regression-002: Historical regression hunt in Prometheus
userStories.description: As a benchmark developer, I want a regression hunt task in Prometheus. Agent must find the commit that introduced a recording rule evaluation regression.
acceptanceCriteria: ground_truth.json includes: regressing commit SHA, changed function, mechanism
userStories.notes: Based on real Prometheus bug (commit a48a01836, June 2017): loadGroups changed map key from 'rg.Name' to 'rg.Name+";" +fn' to allow same group name across files, but ApplyConfig still used 'newg.name', causing state copy to fail. Alert 'for' state and active alerts lost on config reload. Fixed in PR #3382 (commit e86d82ad2, Nov 2017) via groupKey() helper.
